allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Implement JCAMP FxScalper Phase 1 - MT5 EA with pattern fixes

Initial implementation of automated forex scalping EA with the following features:

Core Strategy:
- Trend following using SMA alignment \(21>50>200\)
- RSI momentum confirmation \(>50 for buys, <50 for sells\)
- Dual pattern triggers: Bullish/Bearish Engulfing + Complex 3+1 patterns
- H1 fractal-based support/resistance detection
- Smart TP validation \(75% rule\) and SL snapping to structure

Risk Management:
- Dynamic lot sizing \(1-2% risk per trade\)
- Position limits \(max 1 for EURUSD in Phase 1\)
- Daily loss limit tracking
- Commission-aware break-even calculation
- Partial profit management \(80% at 2:1, 20% runner with RSI divergence\)

Critical Bug Fixes Applied:
1. Pattern indexing: Fixed to check completed bars [1] and [2] instead of incomplete bar [0]
2. Engulfing pattern: Changed strict < to <= for forex \(no gaps between candles\)
3. Engulfing buffer: Added 3-pip buffer for flexibility \(open[1] <= close[2] + buffer\)
4. Input variable sharing: Created JC_Inputs.mqh with include guards to resolve MQL5 compilation errors
5. 3+1 pattern: Removed engulfing requirement from continuation candle
6. Proximity filter: Removed completely per requirements
7. TP validation: Implemented smart 75% rule \(allow if resistance >=75% to target\)

Known Issues:
- No trades generated in 1-year backtest despite pattern fixes
- May require further investigation of entry conditions or market data
- Prepared for conversion to cTrader in next session

Files:
- Main EA: MQL5/Experts/JCAMP_FxScalper_v1.mq5
- Modules: JC_EntryLogic, JC_MarketStructure, JC_RiskManager, JC_TradeManager, JC_Utils
- Common inputs: JC_Inputs.mqh \(include guard pattern for MQL5\)
- Documentation: Docs/PRD, Implementation Log, Testing Guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Fix Phase 1A bugs and update Master Plan architecture

Phase 1A Bug Fixes \(Jcamp_1M_scalping.cs\):
- Fix division by zero in CalculateAverageRange \(count <= 0 guard\)
- Add score clamping to FractalStrength \(Math.Max for lower bound\)
- Add early exit for insufficient bars in FindSignificantSwing

Master Plan Updates:
- Add dual entry mode: Breakout \(default\) + Retest \(alternative\)
- Clarify entry direction: SELL = body below, BUY = body above
- Add dynamic position sizing with risk-based calculation
- Fix FVG detection indexing \(A-B-C pattern\)
- Fix session logic to track independently \(no early return\)
- Add score decomposition logging with configurable weights
- Add hybrid TP logic \(H1 priority + M15 fallback\)
- Document Phase 1A as complete, ready for Phase 1B

Phase 1A validation confirmed all scoring logic is mathematically
correct and strategically sound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Fix build error: DataSeries.Average\(\) API mismatch

cTrader DataSeries.Average\(\) is a LINQ method requiring a selector
function, not a period count like Maximum\(\) and Minimum\(\).

Added CalculateDataSeriesAverage\(\) helper method to manually compute
average over specified periods using series.Last\(i\) accessor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Fix rectangle timing and M1 mode label visibility

Rectangle Timing:
- Changed endTime from swingTime+60 to Server.Time+60
- Rectangle now extends forward from current time, not swing detection time
- Swing bar \(15mins ago\) to NOW+60mins = 75min total coverage

M1 Mode Label:
- Added initial mode detection in OnStart \(shows immediately\)
- Added mode label refresh on every M1 bar \(not just M15 changes\)
- Mode \(BUY/SELL\) now visible on M1 chart continuously

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Fix M1 vs M15 data consistency - use same M15 data source

Issue:
- M15 chart used native Bars, M1 used MarketData.GetBars\(M15\)
- Different bar counts caused different EMA values
- Led to opposite mode detection at same time

Fix:
- Always use MarketData.GetBars\(TimeFrame.Minute15\) for both charts
- EMA 200 always calculated on M15 data \(M1 EMA ignored\)
- Same data source = consistent mode detection

Added debug logging for bar count and last bar time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Switch to SMA for mode detection - fix M1/M15 consistency

Root Cause:
- EMA\(200\) calculation depends on ALL historical data
- Different charts load different amounts of history
- Same time, different EMA values = different modes

Fix:
- Replaced EMA with custom SMA calculation for mode detection
- SMA only uses last N bars \(not affected by total history\)
- CalculateSMA\(\) uses m15Bars.ClosePrices.Last\(i\) for consistency

Result:
- M1 and M15 charts should now detect same mode at same time
- Last 200 M15 bars determine trend, regardless of history depth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C "D:\\JCAMP_FxScalper" commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''

- Add recency-based validity scoring \(recent swings score higher\)
- Fix rectangle end time to use M15 bar time instead of Server.Time
- Add debug output for rectangle timing analysis

Validated:
- M1 and M15 charts now show same mode and rectangles
- Rectangle visual position is correct
- Scoring system properly evaluates swing quality

Ready for Phase 1B: Entry Logic implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(Dual SMA filter failed" repeatedly
- Only yellow pre-zones visible, never armed

FIX #2: Relax Dual SMA to SMA200 only
- BUY: currentPrice > sma200Value \(trend direction\)
- SELL: currentPrice < sma200Value \(trend direction\)
- Allows pullbacks to Fast SMA \(where FVG zones form\)

EXPECTED IMPACT:
- Smaller stop losses \(zone edge, not beyond\)
- Better R:R ratios
- Zones will arm properly \(blue/green zones will appear\)
- Actual rejection-based entries as designed

TEST RESULTS: Baseline backtest showed 422 trades with -$3,767 loss
due to these bugs. Visual inspection confirmed orders outside zones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
